coleen_headline: From the ICU to the Caribbean
coleen_intro: I am Coleen Huie Garcia — registered nurse, grandmother of Taylor and Justin, wife of Ricardo, and the person who finally did what everyone said was too risky.
coleen_bio: After 30 years working in healthcare in North America, I hit a wall. Not the dramatic kind where everything falls apart at once. The slow kind. The kind where you realize one day that you have been running on empty for so long you forgot what full felt like. I made a choice. I moved to the Dominican Republic. Semi-retired at a pace that finally made sense for my life. And I have never looked back.
coleen_credential_4: Wife of Ricardo — DR Police Officer & Expat Consultant
ricardo_role: Dominican Republic Police Officer & Local Expat Consultant
ricardo_bio: Ricardo has served on the Dominican Republic police force for 15 years. He holds a law degree but does not practice in court — instead, he uses that knowledge to help expats understand what they can do themselves, navigate local systems with confidence, and connect with trusted professionals when needed. He has been consulting with expats on a word-of-mouth basis for years and brings an unmatched level of local knowledge and genuine care to every person he works with.
lifestyle_headline: This Is What the Other Side Looks Like
lifestyle_text: Fresh fruit every morning. Ocean views. A pace of life that does not punish you for slowing down. This is not a fantasy. It is Tuesday in the Dominican Republic. And it can be your Tuesday too.
